results.content:
Jan Heimes
Needle

Needle

Mustafa Arian

most ai waits to be asked. this one talks first. that flip is where the trust question gets real. reactive is easy because the worst case is a bad reply. proactive is scary because the worst case is a bad action nobody signed off on. the design job is making sure the actions are the ones the human would have taken 5 minutes later anyway.

curious how you handle disagreement. when needle drafts follow-up and the rep would have written it differently, does the rep edit and move on, or does needle learn to write more like that rep over time?

Valentin

#### What's great

Great tool for building AI workflows . Easy to use, fast, and saves a lot of time. Highly recommended.

#### What needs improvement

It would be great to make importing workflows even easier. For example, allowing n8n users to upload JSON workflow files directly into Needle for faster setup and smoother integration.

results.title: Proactive AI Agents: Definition, Core Components, and Business Value | Slack
results.content:
Yes. Many teams start small. They build on existing workflows, such as “if x, then y” automations, by layering proactive capabilities on top of them. Agentforce agents from Salesforce, for example, help teams define the role and guardrails for an agent to follow, identify opportunities for the agent to expand its learning and capabilities, and customize agents for unique business needs across functions.

 Productivity

### Nice one!

Thanks a lot for your feedback!

### Got it!

Thanks for your feedback.

Whoops! We’re having some problems. Please try again later.

## Keep reading

News

### Introducing Today: Your Intelligent Daily Briefing in Slack

The new Today view shows you what matters most, so you can skip the morning scroll and get straight to work.

Transformation [...] One of the biggest technical challenges in developing proactive agents is balancing autonomy while maintaining safety and trust. A good proactive agent must be trustworthy, assessing the source material and making reasonable suggestions for human teams based on that information. It can be challenging to build an agent that can be proactive, accurate, and secure.

Yes, proactive agents are safe when designed with transparency, governance, and oversight. They act within the parameters set by human teams, executing and supporting what humans tell them to focus on. [...] As with human teams, a feedback loop is necessary for learning, improvement, and optimization. After an action is taken, an effective agent is designed to receive feedback on that action. The feedback then kickstarts its sensing capability, helping ensure that next time, it can provide better assistance.

## Benefits of proactive agents in modern workflows

When applied thoughtfully, proactive agents deliver tangible benefits across day-to-day workplace operations. These common issues are good places to start when considering where proactive agents can have an early impact.

### Reduced manual prompting and fewer errors

results.title: The AI Slack Agent Replacing Newsletters for Modern Teams
results.content:
Daily. Weekly. Every Monday morning before standup. Whenever you ask it directly.

Each automated report includes:

   Key findings.The things that actually moved, broken out clearly
   Summary.Readable in under two minutes, no jargon
   Major Source links.Every claim is linked to the original source.
   PDF file.Formatted, shareable, downloadable file

No dashboard to log into. No email to hunt through. The briefing shows up in the channel your team is already in all day. You can also set it up to show up in your DMs.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Image 4

Daily Report look once it delivers on Slack

Press enter or click to view image in full sizeImage 5

Press enter or click to view image in full sizeImage 6 [...] ### What is included in each automated report?

Every report includes a plain-language summary, key findings broken out clearly, source links for every claim, and a downloadable PDF formatted for sharing or filing.

### How is this different from a newsletter or Google Alerts?

Newsletters cover the broadest audience for a topic; this agent covers the specific topic you define. Google Alerts surface news articles; this agent hits properly indexed sources including SEC filings, PubMed research, clinical trial databases, and regulatory filings. The result is a deepresearch report configured specifically for your decisions, not a general digest.

### Do I need to know how to code? [...] Market intelligence. Teams tracking competitor moves, pricing changes, product launches, and executive hires. The kind of signal that matters for competitive positioning but never gets monitored systematically because nobody has time to do it manually.

Competitor monitoring. Not just news coverage, primary source signals. Job postings that reveal strategic direction. Funding disclosures. Partnership announcements. Regulatory filings competitors had to make.

Regulatory tracking. Compliance teams and legal departments staying current on rule changes, enforcement actions, and guidance documents. Missing a regulatory update because it dropped on a Thursday afternoon is a real operational risk.
